prompt: Tell me one thing I'm probably wrong about — not a hypothetical me, just a stranger writing to you. Pick something.
response: You're probably wrong about the idea that cracking your knuckles causes arthritis. It's a common myth—studies show no link between knuckle-cracking and joint issues; it's just harmless gas bubbles popping in the synovial fluid. If you do it a lot, it might annoy people around you, though!
